regions.us-ports: Today in Miami I have ESE 4kt winds with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, so gangway and tender operations are proceeding within light-wind limits while crews maintain heightened lightning monitoring around the thunderstorms. Over the next 24 hours Miami is forecast ESE 8kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and Port Everglades and Port Canaveral should schedule embarkation windows around the light ESE wind and persistent thunderstorm sky conditions. Between 24 and 48 hours Miami trends to SSE 12kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, which keeps maneuvering and docking within moderate wind parameters for Tampa Bay and adjacent terminals. Beyond 48 hours the forecast shows SE 11kt with thunderstorms and a 37% rain probability for Miami, and that 37% rain probability may affect shore excursions and tender plans so I will issue updated guidance as the SE 11kt winds and thunderstorm development evolve.
